title: Lolium humile
intro: Lolium humile, also known as Ryegrass or annual ryegrass, is an annual grassy plant belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lolium humile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lolium humile is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal problems, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote sweating and relieve fever.

uses: Lolium humile, also known as creeping ryegrass, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
